visa_types.name: Passport Services
visa_types.overview:
Welcome to the website of the Philippines Passport Renewal.Center |(PaRC)
In an effort to ease the passport renewal process for Philippines national overseas, the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) and VFS Global signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) on the establishment of a Passport Renewal Center (PaRC).
PaRC aims to provide an alternative means for Filipinos to avail of passport renewal services in the UK.
PaRC is authorized by the DFA to accept passport applications from Filipinos holding electronic passports and whose applications do not entail a change in passport record of the holder. The Embassy monitors passport applications lodged through PaRC and the passports that are produced go through the Embassy.
This site contains important information on passport renewal process at PaRC. Please go through the contents carefully before proceeding with your application.
- Adult
- Minor
visa_types.full_text:
Welcome to the website of the Philippines Passport Renewal.Center |(PaRC)
In an effort to ease the passport renewal process for Philippines national overseas, the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) and VFS Global signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) on the establishment of a Passport Renewal Center (PaRC).
PaRC aims to provide an alternative means for Filipinos to avail of passport renewal services in the UK.
PaRC is authorized by the DFA to accept passport applications from Filipinos holding electronic passports and whose applications do not entail a change in passport record of the holder. The Embassy monitors passport applications lodged through PaRC and the passports that are produced go through the Embassy.
This site contains important information on passport renewal process at PaRC. Please go through the contents carefully before proceeding with your application.
- Adult
- Minor

visa_types.name: Minor
visa_types.documents_required:
- Personal appearance of the minor applicant at the ePassport Renewal Centre is a must. Minor must be accompanied by either parent.
- Accomplished Online Application Form
             click here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/) or download the form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1UDlmxc3f6EXnhI5ripoSO/0974bebdc4a81707dd3f58b7c4710c3a/New-Application-Minor.pdf)
- Original Passport
- One (1) photocopy, data page of the current Philippine passport.
- Original and one (1) photocopy, PSA-issued Birth Certificate of the minor applicant
- If the minor is an illegitimate child, personal appearance of the mother is required.
- If either parent is unable to appear, the following must be presented:
- A Special Power of Attorney (SPA) authorising a representative to assist the child in the application process
- A copy of either parent’s valid passport
- A copy of the valid passport or photographic ID of the authorized representative
- Original, valid Philippine passports of the parent accompanying the minor applicant
- One (1) photocopy, data page of the accompanying parent’s valid Philippine passport
- Original and one (1) photocopy, PSA-issued Marriage Certificate of parents
Important Instruction for applicant:
- Personal appearance of the child and either parent. If the child’s age is seven (7) years and below, only the mother can accompany the child during passport application.
- If the child is accompanied by his/her father during passport application, the original and one (1) photocopy of the parents’ PSA-issued Marriage Certificate or Report of Marriage must be presented.
visa_types.processing_time: The new passport may be released within at least 8 to 10 weeks from the date the application is submitted, barring unforeseen events
visa_types.overview:
Overview
For Minor Applicants (below 18 years old)
Passport to be issued to a minor child will be valid for 5 years.
A minor applicant must be accompanied by either parent during the time of application.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For Minor Applicants (below 18 years old)
Passport to be issued to a minor child will be valid for 5 years.
A minor applicant must be accompanied by either parent during the time of application. 

## Application Fees

[Table:  Minor]
Please Note:
- Application fee for renewal of passport is payable in cash or debit/credit card. The passport renewal fee is similar to the fee charged at the Philippines Embassy for the renewal of Philippine passports.
- Applicant shall be charged a convenience fee of EUR 52.09 per application in addition to the Passport fees and must be paid in card (all applicable taxes included). We do not accept cash and American Express card. The applicable fee in EUR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable

## Documents Required
- Personal appearance of the minor applicant at the ePassport Renewal Centre is a must. Minor must be accompanied by either parent.
- Accomplished Online Application Form
             click here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/) or download the form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1UDlmxc3f6EXnhI5ripoSO/0974bebdc4a81707dd3f58b7c4710c3a/New-Application-Minor.pdf)
- Original Passport
- One (1) photocopy, data page of the current Philippine passport.
- Original and one (1) photocopy, PSA-issued Birth Certificate of the minor applicant
- If the minor is an illegitimate child, personal appearance of the mother is required.
- If either parent is unable to appear, the following must be presented:
- A Special Power of Attorney (SPA) authorising a representative to assist the child in the application process
- A copy of either parent’s valid passport
- A copy of the valid passport or photographic ID of the authorized representative
- Original, valid Philippine passports of the parent accompanying the minor applicant
- One (1) photocopy, data page of the accompanying parent’s valid Philippine passport
- Original and one (1) photocopy, PSA-issued Marriage Certificate of parents
Important Instruction for applicant:
- Personal appearance of the child and either parent. If the child’s age is seven (7) years and below, only the mother can accompany the child during passport application.
- If the child is accompanied by his/her father during passport application, the original and one (1) photocopy of the parents’ PSA-issued Marriage Certificate or Report of Marriage must be presented.

## Photo Specifications
Passport photograph will be captured at the Passport Renewal Centre.
Important Reminders:
Eyeglasses, contact lenses, jewelry (earring, necklace), heavy make-up should be removed before photo capturing.  It is also advisable for applicants having sore eyes, eye sty or any redness in the eyes to come for e-passport renewal when their eye condition improves

## Processing Time
The new passport may be released within at least 8 to 10 weeks from the date the application is submitted, barring unforeseen events

## Application Form
To complete your online application form click here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/)
OR
Click here to download the form and click print to confirm printing

visa_types.name: Adult
visa_types.documents_required:
- Personal appearance of the applicant at the Passport ePassport Renewal Centre is required
- Accomplished Online Application Form  
                                                            click here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/) or download the form.
- Original Passport
- One (1) photocopy of the data page of the most recent Philippine passport. To see what it looks like, click here.
- Supporting documents for dual citizens by RA 9225:
- Original and one (1) photocopy of the following:
- Oath of Allegiance
- Order of Approval
- Identification Certificate
visa_types.processing_time: The new passport may be released within at least 8 to 10 weeks from the date the application is submitted, barring unforeseen events
visa_types.overview:
Overview
For Adult Applicants (18 Years old and above)
The Passport Renewal Centre can assist any applicants wishing to renew their ePassports as long as there are no changes on the details of the biographical page. Passports issued to adults will be valid for 10 years.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For Adult Applicants (18 Years old and above)
The Passport Renewal Centre can assist any applicants wishing to renew their ePassports as long as there are no changes on the details of the biographical page. Passports issued to adults will be valid for 10 years.

## Application Fees

[Table:  Adult]
Please Note:
- Application fee for renewal of passport is payable in cash or debit/credit card. The passport renewal fee is similar to the fee charged at the Philippines Embassy for the renewal of Philippine passports.
- Applicant shall be charged a convenience fee of  EUR 52.09 per application in addition to the Passport fees and must be paid in  debit/credit card (all applicable taxes included). We do not accept cash and American Express card. The applicable fee in  EUR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable

## Documents Required
- Personal appearance of the applicant at the Passport ePassport Renewal Centre is required
- Accomplished Online Application Form  
                                                            click here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/) or download the form.
- Original Passport
- One (1) photocopy of the data page of the most recent Philippine passport. To see what it looks like, click here.
- Supporting documents for dual citizens by RA 9225:
- Original and one (1) photocopy of the following:
- Oath of Allegiance
- Order of Approval
- Identification Certificate

## Photo Specifications
Passport photograph will be captured at the Passport Renewal Centre.
Important Reminders:
Eyeglasses, contact lenses, jewelry (earring, necklace), heavy make-up should be removed before photo capturing.  It is also advisable for applicants having sore eyes, eye sty or any redness in the eyes to come for e-passport renewal when their eye condition improves

## Processing Time
The new passport may be released within at least 8 to 10 weeks from the date the application is submitted, barring unforeseen events

## Application Form
To complete your online application form click here (https://onlineappform.passport.gov.ph/)
OR
Click here to download the form and click print to confirm printing
